value_proposition: Help solo developers run a structured agile workflow without a team, by automating the four phases through Claude Code.
scope_boundaries: No real-time collaboration with multiple developers
No native integration with Jira API (paste-based handoff only)
open_assumptions: Users will confirm phase gates manually

stories.statement: As a solo developer, I want the intake subagent to surface ambiguities, so that I don't waste a sprint on a misunderstood brief.
acceptance_criteria: Given two interpretations of user input / When intake runs / Then both interpretations are listed and user is prompted to pick
Given a complete, unambiguous input / When intake runs / Then the validated intake block is emitted with no clarification request

stories.statement: As a maintainer, I want CRITICAL patches flagged before they reach me, so that I don't accidentally approve a patch that breaks the next sprint.
acceptance_criteria: Given a patch with an unresolved assumption / When retro-challenge runs / Then severity is set to CRITICAL and challenge_status is added to the patch
Given a CRITICAL patch / When retro-improve is dispatched / Then retro-improve halts and reports the unresolved patch

architectural_guardrails: Clean Core: phase state lives in .claude/state.json; no business logic in slash-command markdown
Anti-pattern: agents must never write to CLAUDE.md directly
integration_flags: Schema instance must validate against schema/handoff-schema.json after every subagent emission
sprint_goals: Ship working /vision → /retro loop with one end-to-end example committed in schema/examples/

challenge_log.dangerous_assumption: Solo developers will pause to answer break questions instead of skipping them.
break_questions: Have you used a workflow tool you regularly skipped steps in?
Would a forced halt make you abandon the tool entirely?

challenge_log.dangerous_assumption: The Handoff Schema fields are sufficient to express retro patch state without a separate patches table.
break_questions: What happens to FLAGGED patches that need to persist across sprints?
How are challenge_status values updated after the human approves a patch?
challenge_log.resolution: Added retro.flagged_patches and challenge_status field to patch shape.

challenge_log.dangerous_assumption: Two stories will deliver the sprint goal in one iteration.
break_questions: Has anyone on the team built a Claude Code plugin end-to-end before?
Is the schema validation script truly a half-day task or a one-week task?
challenge_log.resolution: Estimate confirmed; risk acknowledged in next_sprint_flags.

improvement_log: P2: merged duplicate acceptance criteria across S1 and S2
P3: removed redundant 'definition of done' prose now captured in dor_status

next_sprint_flags: WATCH: schema validation script estimate may be optimistic — track actual hours vs estimate
